hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 to improve complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity binders but not sufficiently selective ones. A shift in focus to non-pharmacophore positions to break off-target interactions is necessary to achieve the desired SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving SSTR2 pharmacophore.
These mutations are expected to break conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore at positions 7-10.
